body: PT Bumi Serpong Damai TBK (BSDE) acquired a 91.99% stake in PT Suryamas Dutamakmur TBK after purchasing 4.39 billion shares, while PT Eco Energi Perkasa acquired a 49% stake in PT Solusi Kemasan Digital TBK by investing 753.40 million shares. Other notable transactions included DBS Bank Ltd's acquisition of 15 million shares of PT Lippo Karawaci TBK, and Silvery Moon Investments' sale of 187.50 million shares of PT VKTR Teknologi Mobilitas TBK.
title: Multiple Indonesian companies see changes in ownership structure following significant share transactions.

body: PT MD Entertainment Tbk (MDEN) will issue 10% of its shares through a pre-emptive rights offering to strengthen its capital structure and support operational activities, with the approval of an extraordinary general meeting of shareholders scheduled for November 28, 2024. The company's stock price stood at IDR 3,080 per share as of 10:52 am WIB, with PT MD Global Investments holding 50.50% of the company, followed by Manoj Dhamoo Punjabi, Morgan Stanley, and public investors.
title: PT MD Entertainment to issue 10% shares through pre-emptive rights to strengthen capital structure.

body: PT Samuel Tumbuh Bersama reduced its stake in PT Nusantara Sawit Sejahtera Tbk (NSSA) to 25.48% from 25.64% after releasing 38 million shares, while DBS Bank Ltd S/A PT Bank Keb Hana Indonesia sold 10 million shares of PT Lippo Karawaci Tbk (LPKR). Meanwhile, PT Sinar Mas Cakrawala sold 7.55 million shares of PT Aman Agrindo Tbk (AGRO), and PT Startel Communication acquired 700,000 shares of PT Jaya Trishindo Tbk (JAYA), among other transactions.
title: Multiple investors and companies engage in share transactions in various Indonesian companies, including those in palm oil, property development, and shrimp processing.

body: Foreign investors sold a net 477.89 million shares of PT Goto Gojek Tokopedia Tbk (GOTO) on Thursday, October 24, with significant selling also seen in PT Lippo Karawaci Tbk (LPKR), PT Bank Raya Indonesia Tbk (BBRI), and PT Bank Rakyat Indonesia Tbk (BBRI). Meanwhile, foreign investors purchased a net 131 million shares of PT Buana Lintas Lautan Tbk (BULL), 75.85 million shares of PT Toba Bara Sejahtera Tbk (TOBA), and 61.19 million shares of PT Bank Pembangunan Daerah Banten Tbk (BEKS).
title: Foreign investors sell majority of shares in Indonesian companies, led by PT Goto Gojek Tokopedia TBK, on Thursday, October 24.

body: PT Industri Jamu dan Herbal Sido Muncul Tbk (SIDO) is allocating an interim dividend of IDR 540 billion from its net profit in H1 2024, with a dividend per share of IDR 18, based on its net profit of IDR 608.48 billion and unrestricted retained earnings of IDR 994.41 billion. The company has scheduled the cum-ex dividend dates for November 4-5, 2024, and November 6-7, 2024, with payment to be made on November 20, 2024, referring to the recording date of official registers of shareholders on November 6, 2024.
title: PT Industri Jamu dan Herbal Sido Muncul to distribute IDR 540 billion in interim dividends from H1 2024 net profit.

body: PT Global Digital Niaga Tbk (Beli) raised IDR 2.25 trillion in fresh funds through a private placement, issuing 4.9 billion new shares at IDR 460 each, with PT Lingkarmulia Indah taking over the entire new shares. The proceeds will be allocated towards working capital to expand Beli's business, with total issued and paid-up capital increasing by 3.98% to IDR 32.02 trillion.
title: Blibli.com's parent company raises IDR 2.25 trillion in private placement to expand business.

body: PT Wilmar Cahaya Indonesia Tbk (CEKA) reported a net profit of IDR 218.06 billion for the first nine months of 2024, a 78.6% year-over-year increase from IDR 122.09 billion in the same period last year, driven by an 18% increase in revenue to IDR 5.56 trillion and a surge in interest income to IDR 20.75 billion. The company's total assets and equity stood at IDR 2.23 trillion and IDR 1.8 trillion, respectively, as of September 30, 2024.
title: Wilmar Cahaya Indonesia's net profit surges 78.6% to IDR 218.06 billion in the first nine months of 2024.

body: PT Bank Central Asia Tbk (BBCA) reported IDR 1.12 quadrillion in third-party funds as of Q3 2024, a 3.4% year-over-year increase, and achieved a net profit of IDR 41.1 trillion. The bank's credit distribution reached IDR 877 trillion, up 14.5% year-over-year, with a credit structure comprising corporate, commercial, SME, consumption, and other credits.
title: Bank Central Asia's third-party funds reach IDR 1.12 quadrillion, net profit IDR 41.1 trillion in Q3 2024.

body: PT Bank Negara Indonesia Tbk (BNI) through its subsidiary, BNI Venture, introduced 10 startups at the Axel Arc Pitching Demo Day accelerator program, aiming to strengthen its position in the financial services industry through collaboration and innovation. The program, part of BNV Arcade 2024, connects BNI with startups presenting innovative solutions, driving innovation and competitiveness in the banking industry, and creating sustainable added value for both BNI and the startup ecosystem in Indonesia.
title: BNI Introduces 10 Startups through Axel Arc Accelerator Program to Strengthen Innovation in Financial Services.

body: Ferry Saputra, an individual investor, increased his stake in PT Dewi Shri Farmindo TBK (DEWI) to 6.3% by acquiring 19.40 million shares, while PT Karya Nusa Perdana increased its stake in PT Maxindo Karya Anugerah TBK (MKAT) to an undisclosed percentage by acquiring 10.37 million shares. Meanwhile, PT Samuel Tumbuh Bersama recorded the largest share reduction, selling 42.29 million shares of PT Nusantara Sawit Sejahtera TBK (NSSS), and Andreas Utomo sold 14.80 million shares of PT Aman Agrindo TBK (AMAN).
title: Multiple investors make significant transactions in Indonesian companies, including purchases and sales of shares in various industries.

body: PT Tower Bersama Infrastructure Tbk (TBIG) has signed an agreement to relax the tenor of its USD 325 million syndicated loan facility, extending the average tenor with a final settlement date set for October 2029. The revolving loan facility, obtained in April 2023 from a syndicate of banks including BNP Paribas, PT Bank CIMB Niaga Tbk, Credit Agricole Corporate Investment Bank, and others, has an interest rate ranging from 1.25% to 1.48% per annum depending on the senior leverage ratio.
title: PT Tower Bersama Infrastructure TBK signs agreement to extend syndicated loan facility worth USD 325 million.

body: PT Asahimas Flat Glass Tbk (ASMG) is expanding into warehouse leasing by repurposing its idle glass factory in Ancol, North Jakarta, with a land area of 395,528 m2 and building area of 64,037 m2, aiming to generate profits within three years. The company will hold an extraordinary general meeting on November 29, 2024, to seek approval for this new business line expansion, which is expected to bring in revenue after a 5-year effort to market the asset.
title: Asahimas Flat Glass to Expand into Warehouse Leasing Business at Former Factory Site in North Jakarta.

body: PT GoTo Gojek Tokopedia Tbk (GOTO) saw 4.75 billion shares purchased, contributing to a net foreign surplus of 1.63 billion shares, while foreign investors also bought significant shares of PT Bumi Resources Minerals Tbk (BUMI) and PT Topindo Solusi Komunika Tbk (TOPS). Meanwhile, foreign investors sold more shares than they purchased in companies such as PT Wir Asia Tbk (WIRA), PT Bank Rakyat Indonesia Tbk (BBRI), and PT Sinergi Inti Andalan Prima Tbk (SIAP), resulting in net volumes of -78.67 million, -75.91 million, and -60.65 million shares, respectively.
title: Foreign investors purchase 7.41 billion shares in Indonesian market, led by PT Goto Gojek Tokopedia TBK and PT Bumi Resources Minerals TBK.

body: Foreign investors purchased a net 1.02 billion shares, with digital company PT Goto Gojek Tokopedia Tbk (GOTO) leading the buying list with 2.06 billion shares, while e-commerce company PT Bukalapak.com Tbk (BUKA) topped the selling list with 308.25 million shares. Other notable transactions included PT Buana Lintas Lautan Tbk (BULL) with 141.23 million shares purchased, and PT Bumi Resources Tbk (BUMI) with 151.16 million shares sold.
title: Foreign investors buy 1.02 billion more shares than they sell in Indonesian market, led by digital company GoTo.
